sn47.11:1.3: The thirty-two “marks of a great man” are detailed in DN 14:1.32.7, DN 30:1.2.4, and MN 91:9.1, while the “meditation of a great man” is said to be emptiness (MN 151:2.6), and the eight “thoughts of a great man” at detailed at AN 8.30 and DN 34:2.1.150. | Sāriputta is asking the Buddha to redefine a Vedic term. Śāṅkhāyana Āraṇyaka 8.3 says: “The great man is just the year, of which the sun is the essence. That which is the incorporeal self made of wisdom, and that which is yonder sun are one and the same: so should one understand it.” Thus for the Vedists, the great man is the realized soul who understands that he is essentially one with the divine cosmos.
